title: Business Continuity Program
body_text:
Business Continuity Program
1	Introduction
1.1	Overview of the Business Continuity Program
Central 1 has established a comprehensive Business Continuity Program (BCP) consisting of the following:
Policies for business continuity
The definition of roles and responsibilities
Procedures for information security
An assessment of business continuity risk
An analysis of business impact
Testing of the business continuity and disaster recovery plans, and
Maintenance of the business continuity and disaster recovery plans
Within the Central 1 BCP, the Business Continuity Plans are intended to address a disaster at Central 1. A âdisasterâ means a Central 1 system disaster, such as a single central system or component failure; a Central 1 site disaster, such as a portion of or the entire Central 1 office building becoming uninhabitable or un-operable; or a regional disaster, such as a natural or man-made disaster affecting a wide geographical area.
1.2	Business Continuity Plans
The Central 1 Business Continuity Plans include the following plans:
Plan 1 â Crisis Management & Communications (CMC)
The objective of the CMC is to activate members of Central 1âs executive management, business continuity management, communications, and business recovery teams during a crisis. These members facilitate a disaster declaration and communication to staff, clients, vendors, regulators, government agencies, partners, media, and other key stakeholders.
Plan 2 â Corporate Incident Response Plan (IRP)
The IRP consists of cross-functional teams that provide an immediate and coordinated response to incidents as a result of disasters that could negatively impact Central 1âs staff, property, or services. The size and scope of a disaster, as assessed solely by Central 1, will establish the size and scope of the teams activated.
Plan 3 â Technology Recovery Plan (TRP)
The TRP follows Central 1âs policies and procedures for technology disaster recovery, as well as the process-level plan for recovering critical technology platforms and telecommunications infrastructure.
Plan 4 â Pandemic Response Plan (PRP)
The PRP ensures the continuity of all Central 1âs critical functions during a staff shortage due to a declared pandemic.
1.3	Business Continuity Programâs Key Components
Central 1 Main Switch Board
Central 1âs main switch board number is
1-800-661-6813
. During a disaster, the same number will remain active, via an out of region virtual switch board. Central 1âs main reception desk will continue its operation at Central 1âs alternate regional location.
Alert and Assemble Recovery Team
Central 1 will use commercially reasonable efforts to contact its clients to place them on alert when Central 1 declares a disaster situation. When a disaster is declared, Central 1âs efforts to start recovery of the services will begin immediately.
Recovery
System Recoverability
Central 1 maintains multiple data centers, including one in Vancouver and Toronto. Each data center maintains multiple internet connectivity points, uninterruptible power supplies and power generators.
In the rare event that both the Vancouver and Toronto data centers are out of service, Central 1âs services will not be available to its clients. Central 1 has never lost both data centers supporting the services at once.
Based on the service level of individual Central 1 systems, a specific operating mode will be designated. The following operating modes are contained in the BCP:
i) Mission Critical â If a service is categorized as mission critical and is running from both Central 1 data centers, little or no intervention is required by Central 1 to restore its services. These services rely on domain name servers for load balancing and automatic failover purposes as well as its clientsâ customer web-browser settings. Central 1 clientsâ customers may experience a propagation delay from the moment a service fails in one of Central 1 data centers to such service being provided by Central 1âs alternate data center. Depending on the conditions set out above in this Section, the disaster magnitude, and Central 1 staff availability, it may take between zero minutes and 4 hours for the services to be recovered from the moment a disaster is declared by Central 1, to the time the services are considered recovered by Central 1.
ii) Business Critical â If a service categorized as business critical, it is deployed in both Central 1 data centers and manual intervention is required by Central 1 to restore services. Depending on the disaster magnitude and Central 1 staff availability, it may take between 15 minutes and 48 hours from the moment a disaster is declared by Central 1, to the time the services are considered recovered by Central 1.
iii) Business Important â If a service categorized as business important, it is deployed in both Central 1 data centers and manual intervention is required by Central 1 to restore services. Depending on the disaster magnitude and Central 1 staff availability, it may take between 4 hours and 30 days from the moment a disaster is declared by Central 1, to the time the services are considered recovered by Central 1.
iv) Business Supporting â If a service categorized as business supporting is not deployed in both Central 1 data centers, the system may need to be recreated from backup to restore the services. Depending on the disaster magnitude and Central 1 staff availability, it may take between 48 hours and more than 30 days from the moment a disaster is declared by Central 1, to the time the services are considered recovered by Central 1.
Mobile Backup Facilities
Central 1 maintains a mobile backup trailer arrangement with a third party. The mobile backup provides Central 1 with basic facilities for Central 1 to continue its operations. The mobile backup will be available to Central 1 within 48 hours of a Central 1 request. The mobile backup will be equipped with satellite based voice and data connectivity, power generator and staff facilities for the ongoing operation of Central 1 services.
Impact on Services
While Central 1âs systems may recover and be available, Central 1âs clients will require backup connections from its clientsâ systems to the Central 1 systems. Specifically, Central 1âs clients will need to maintain a direct connection from their systems to both Central 1 data centers to ensure minimal service disruption to their services.
Other Central 1 services provided to our clients that do not impact the normal day to day access of services by the clientsâ customers, including, but not limited to Change Requests, will be impacted during a disaster, until Central 1 can make alternative arrangements for staff and resources. Within 48 hours of the declaration of a disaster by Central 1, Central 1 will use commercially reasonable efforts to update its clients on the status of its recovery efforts and advise when other services will resume normal operation.
